title: Add image comparison metrics, tolerance, options pattern, and difference image generation
body: ## Summary Adds standard image comparison metrics, tolerance-based comparison, an extensible options pattern (`SKPixelComparerOptions`), alpha channel comparison, and per-channel difference image generation to `SKPixelComparer`. ## New metrics on `SKPixelComparisonResult` - `SumSquaredError` (long) — Σ(ΔR² + ΔG² + ΔB²) - `MeanAbsoluteError` — per-channel MAE, range [0, 255] - `MeanSquaredError` — per-channel MSE - `RootMeanSquaredError` — RMSE (the metric MAUI's `MagickNetVisualComparer` uses) - `NormalizedRootMeanSquaredError` — RMSE / 255, range [0, 1] for threshold checks - `PeakSignalToNoiseRatio` — PSNR in dB - `ChannelCount` — 3 (RGB) or 4 (RGBA), used to normalize MAE/MSE ## SKPixelComparerOptions Extensible options class replacing boolean parameters: ```csharp var options = new SKPixelComparerOptions { TolerancePerChannel = true, // Check each channel independently (default) CompareAlpha = true // Include alpha in comparison (default: false) }; var result = SKPixelComparer.Compare(expected, actual, options); ``` Options work with all comparison modes (base, tolerance, mask) and all input types (file paths, `SKImage`, `SKBitmap`, `SKPixmap`). ## Tolerance-based comparison Like ImageMagick's `fuzz` — a uniform per-pixel threshold without needing a full mask image: ```csharp var result = SKPixelComparer.Compare(expected, actual, tolerance: 10); ``` ## Per-channel difference image `GenerateDifferenceImage` produces actual RGB difference values per pixel (vs. the binary B/W `GenerateDifferenceMask`): ```csharp using var diff = SKPixelComparer.GenerateDifferenceImage(expected, actual); ``` ## Bug fixes - **Premultiplied alpha**: `GetNormalizedBitmap` now uses `SKAlphaType.Unpremul` so semi-transparent pixels have correct RGB values - **Platform color type**: Diff images are built in BGRA8888 (matching `SKColor` memory layout) then converted to platform default via canvas draw - **Resource leak**: `resultBitmap` in `GenerateDifferenceMask`/`GenerateDifferenceImage` is now properly disposed - **Divide-by-zero**: Metrics return 0 when `TotalPixels` is 0 ## Other changes - Nullable annotations enabled (`#nullable enable`) on all comparer files - `Compare(SKImage, SKImage)` and `Compare(SKImage, SKImage, options)` delegate to the tolerance overload (no duplicated loops) - Full XML documentation on all public APIs - Comprehensive user-facing docs at `docs/docs/pixel-comparer.md` ## Backward compatibility - Existing `SKPixelComparisonResult` constructors preserved (3-param and 4-param chain to new 5-param with `channelCount: 3`) - All original `Compare` method signatures unchanged ## Blazor sample The Pixel Comparer demo page showcases metrics display, tolerance slider, and difference image visualization with responsive layout. ## Tests 188 tests across 8 focused test files: | File | Tests | Coverage | |------|-------|----------| | SKPixelComparerCompareTest | 19 | Base comparison, metrics, null args, file overloads | | SKPixelComparerToleranceTest | 16 | Tolerance, per-channel, boundary, max value | | SKPixelComparerMaskTest | 9 | Mask comparison, bitmap/pixmap overloads, dimension mismatch | | SKPixelComparerOptionsTest | 9 | Options pattern, CompareAlpha, semi-transparent premul | | SKPixelComparerDifferenceImageTest | 7 | Per-channel diff, null args, size validation | | SKPixelComparerDifferenceMaskTest | 3 | Binary mask, null args, size validation | | SKColorLayoutTest | 4 | Platform color type, encode roundtrip verification | | BlurHash tests | 121 | Pre-existing (unchanged) |

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body: @copilot+claude-opus-4.6 please review these code changes and make sure we are doing it right.

comments.author: github-actions[bot]
comments.body:

comments.author: Copilot
comments.body:
> @copilot+claude-opus-4.6 please review these code changes and make sure we are doing it right.

I reviewed the full diff across all 4 changed files. Here's a summary:

**Metrics (SKPixelComparisonResult)** — All formulas are mathematically correct and follow standard definitions used by ImageMagick and MAUI's `MagickNetVisualComparer`:
- MAE/MSE normalize by `TotalPixels × 3` (per-channel average)
- PSNR uses standard `10 × log₁₀(MAX² / MSE)` with `∞` for identical images
- `SumSquaredError` uses `long` to prevent overflow on large images

**Tolerance-based comparison** — Semantics match ImageMagick's fuzz: pixels with `|ΔR|+|ΔG|+|ΔB| ≤ tolerance` are excluded from all metrics. `tolerance=0` is verified equivalent to standard comparison via test.

**Backward compatibility** — Old 3-param `SKPixelComparisonResult` constructor preserved, chains to new 4-param constructor with `sumSquaredError: 0`.

**`GenerateDifferenceImage`** — Follows same pattern as existing `GenerateDifferenceMask`.

**Tests** — 22 new tests covering metric correctness (boundary + formula), tolerance edge cases, and diff image. All 63 tests pass (41 original + 22 new).

No issues found — the implementation looks correct.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body: @copilot the tolerance slider does not seem to do anything in the sample.

comments.author: Copilot
comments.body:
> @copilot the tolerance slider does not seem to do anything in the sample. 

Fixed in ff1ffdc. Two issues:

1. The slider was using `onchange` (Blazor default), which only fires on mouse release — switched to `@bind:event="oninput"` so it updates in real-time while dragging.
2. The max tolerance was 100, but the PNG pair has mostly large pixel differences (calculator "0" vs "5"), so tolerance=100 only filtered ~6% of errors. Increased max to 765 (theoretical max `|ΔR|+|ΔG|+|ΔB|`), so the slider now visibly reduces errors from 12,570 → 902 at tolerance=400.

![Tolerance slider at 400](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/db1e5bb5-6704-4afa-b483-a441abc21e8e)

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body: @copilot please now review the final diff and give me a good commit message.

comments.author: Copilot
comments.body:
> @copilot please now review the final diff and give me a good commit message. 

I reviewed the full diff across all changed files. Everything looks correct:

**Source (`SKPixelComparisonResult`)** — New 4-param constructor with `SumSquaredError`, six derived metric properties (MAE/MSE/RMSE/NRMSE/PSNR), backward-compatible 3-param constructor chains to it. All XML-documented.

**Source (`SKPixelComparer`)** — SSE computed in both standard and tolerance-based `Compare`. Four tolerance overloads (string/SKBitmap/SKPixmap/SKImage) with proper validation. Four `GenerateDifferenceImage` overloads following the same pattern as `GenerateDifferenceMask`. All XML-documented.

**Tests** — 22 new tests covering metric formulas, tolerance edge cases (0, partial, full), diff image output, and boundary conditions. No issues.

**Blazor sample** — Tolerance slider with `oninput` + max 765, difference image panel, responsive grid (`col-6 col-lg-3`), all 8 metrics with sub-notes, image caching to avoid re-fetch on tolerance change.

**Docs** — `pixel-comparer.md` updated with new metrics, tolerance, and diff image sections.

No issues found. Here's the suggested squash commit message:

```
Add image comparison metrics, tolerance-based compare, and difference image

Add standard metrics to SKPixelComparisonResult: SumSquaredError, MAE, MSE,
RMSE, NRMSE, and PSNR. Add tolerance-based Compare overloads that exclude
pixels where |ΔR|+|ΔG|+|ΔB| ≤ tolerance. Add GenerateDifferenceImage for
per-channel RGB diff visualization.

Update Blazor sample with interactive tolerance slider, difference image
panel, all 8 metrics with descriptions, and responsive mobile layout.
Add 22 new tests and update documentation.
```

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
## Image Comparison Library Survey

I researched 6 popular image comparison libraries to see how our `SKPixelComparer` (after PR #374) stacks up. Here's the feature matrix:

### Metrics Comparison

| Feature / Metric | **SKPixelComparer** (ours) | **ImageMagick** `compare` | **Pixelmatch** (Mapbox) | **Resemble.js** (Huddle) | **ImageSharp.Compare** (Codeuctivity) | **Blink-Diff** (Yahoo) | **LooksSame** (Gemini) |
|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| **Error Pixel Count** | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ (return value) | ✅ | ✅ `PixelErrorCount` | ✅ `differences` | ✅ `differentPixels` |
| **Error Pixel %** | ✅ | — | — | ✅ `misMatchPercentage` | ✅ `PixelErrorPercentage` | — (manual) | — |
| **Absolute Error** | ✅ | ✅ (AE metric) | — | — | ✅ `AbsoluteError` | — | — |
| **Mean Absolute Error (MAE)** | ✅ | ✅ (MAE metric) | — | — | ✅ `MeanError` | — | — |
| **Mean Squared Error (MSE)** | ✅ | ✅ (MSE metric) | — | — | — | — | — |
| **Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE)** | ✅ | ✅ (RMSE metric, default) | — | — | — | — | — |
| **Normalized RMSE (NRMSE)** | ✅ | ✅ (normalized output) | — | — | — | — | — |
| **Peak Signal-to-Noise (PSNR)** | ✅ | ✅ (PSNR metric) | — | — | — | — | — |
| **Sum Squared Error (SSE)** | ✅ | — | — | — | — | — | — |
| **Structural Similarity (SSIM)** | — | ✅ (SSIM/DSSIM) | — | — | — | — | — |
| **Normalized Cross-Correlation (NCC)** | — | ✅ (NCC metric) | — | — | — | — | — |
| **Perceptual Hash (PHASH)** | — | ✅ (PHASH metric) | — | — | — | — | — |
| **Fidelity (FI)** | — | ✅ (FI metric) | — | — | — | — | — |

### Feature Comparison

| Feature | **SKPixelComparer** | **ImageMagick** | **Pixelmatch** | **Resemble.js** | **ImageSharp.Compare** | **Blink-Diff** | **LooksSame** |
|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| **Tolerance / Fuzz** | ✅ per-pixel `\|ΔR\|+\|ΔG\|+\|ΔB\| ≤ t` | ✅ `-fuzz` (color distance) | ✅ perceptual threshold (0–1) | ✅ (configurable) | ✅ (via mask image) | ✅ `delta` (4D color distance) | ✅ `tolerance` (ΔE) |
| **Tolerance Mask Image** | ✅ per-channel mask | ✅ (via read-mask) | — | — | ✅ (core feature) | — | — |
| **Anti-aliasing Detection** | — | — | ✅ (core feature) | ✅ `ignoreAntialiasing` | — | ✅ h/v shift detection | ✅ `ignoreAntialiasing` |
| **Perceptual Color (ΔE / YIQ)** | — | ✅ (multiple color spaces) | ✅ YIQ NTSC | — | — | ✅ (perceptual mode) | ✅ CIE76 ΔE |
| **Diff Mask (binary B/W)** | ✅ `GenerateDifferenceMask` | — | ✅ `diffMask` option | — | ✅ `CalcDiffMaskImage` | — | — |
| **Diff Image (RGB channel deltas)** | ✅ `GenerateDifferenceImage` | ✅ (default output) | — | — | — | — | — |
| **Highlighted Diff (overlay)** | — | ✅ (red/white highlight) | ✅ (configurable colors) | ✅ (configurable colors) | — | ✅ (composition view) | ✅ (configurable highlight) |
| **Diff Bounds / Clusters** | — | — | — | ✅ `diffBounds` | — | — | ✅ `diffBounds` + `diffClusters` |
| **Bounding Box / Region-of-Interest** | — | ✅ (crop + subimage) | — | ✅ `boundingBox` | — | ✅ `cropImageA/B` | — |
| **Block-out / Ignore Regions** | — | — | — | ✅ `ignoredBox`, `ignoreAreasColoredWith` | — | ✅ `blockOut` | — |
| **Subimage Search** | — | ✅ (with FFT acceleration) | — | — | — | — | — |
| **Alpha Channel Support** | — | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | — (ignores alpha) | ✅ | ✅ |
| **Caret Ignoring** | — | — | — | — | — | — | ✅ `ignoreCaret` |
| **Per-channel Reporting** | — | ✅ (R/G/B/all) | — | ✅ (R/G/B/A/brightness) | — | — | — |
| **Scale-to-Same-Size** | — | — | — | ✅ `scaleToSameSize` | — | — | — |

### Summary

**What we do well (after PR #374):**
- **Most comprehensive metrics suite** among pixel-level comparers — we match ImageMagick's core metrics (MAE, MSE, RMSE, NRMSE, PSNR) while being a lightweight .NET library
- **Both mask types** (binary B/W mask + per-channel RGB diff image) — only ImageMagick also offers both
- **Tolerance mask image** support — only shared with ImageMagick and ImageSharp.Compare
- **Uniform tolerance** — similar to ImageMagick's fuzz

**Gaps vs. the field (potential future work):**
1. **SSIM (Structural Similarity)** — ImageMagick's most "advanced" metric; considers luminance, contrast, and structure rather than raw pixel values. Widely used in academic/quality assessment contexts.
2. **Anti-aliasing detection** — Pixelmatch, Resemble.js, Blink-Diff, and LooksSame all handle this. Critical for visual regression testing where rendering engines produce slightly different AA.
3. **Perceptual color difference (ΔE / YIQ)** — LooksSame and Pixelmatch use human-perception color spaces. More meaningful than raw RGB distance for "do these look the same to a human?"
4. **Highlighted diff overlay** — Most JS libraries overlay differences on the original image with configurable highlight colors. We only output raw diffs.
5. **Diff bounds / clusters** — LooksSame and Resemble.js can report *where* differences are located, not just *how many*.
6. **Block-out / ignore regions** — Blink-Diff and Resemble.js can exclude dynamic areas (timestamps, IDs) from comparison.
7. **Alpha channel** — We currently compare RGB only; several libraries include alpha in the comparison.

### Ecosystem Context

| Library | Language | Dependencies | Standalone |
|---|---|---|:---:|
| **SKPixelComparer** | C# (.NET) | SkiaSharp | ✅ |
| **ImageMagick** | C (CLI/bindings) | Native binary | ✅ |
| **Pixelmatch** | JavaScript | None (150 LOC) | ✅ |
| **Resemble.js** | JavaScript | node-canvas (Node) | ✅ |
| **ImageSharp.Compare** | C# (.NET) | SixLabors.ImageSharp | ✅ |
| **Blink-Diff** | JavaScript | pngjs-image | ✅ |
| **LooksSame** | JavaScript | sharp | ✅ |

Our `SKPixelComparer` is the most feature-rich pure-.NET/SkiaSharp option. ImageSharp.Compare is the closest .NET competitor but lacks advanced metrics (MSE, RMSE, PSNR). ImageMagick is the gold standard but requires native binaries.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
## Code Review: PR #374 — 3-Way Review (Code, API, Docs)

I performed a thorough review across code correctness, API design, and documentation quality.

---

### 🔴 Code Issues

#### 1. `AbsoluteError` (`int`) overflows on ≥1440p images

This is **pre-existing** but worth fixing in this PR since `SumSquaredError` was correctly added as `long`.

Max per-pixel diff = 765 (`255×3`). For a 2560×1440 image: `765 × 3,686,400 = 2,820,096,000` — **overflows `int.MaxValue` (2,147,483,647)**. Even 1920×1440 is borderline.

`SumSquaredError` is already `long`. `AbsoluteError` should be too. This is a **binary breaking change** but the current behavior silently wraps to negative values on large images, which is worse.

**Recommendation:** Change `AbsoluteError` from `int` to `long` in both `SKPixelComparisonResult` and the accumulator variables in `SKPixelComparer.Compare`. Keep the old 3-param constructor signature accepting `int` for source compat.

#### 2. `diffBitmap` is never disposed in `GenerateDifferenceMask` and `GenerateDifferenceImage`

```csharp
var diffBitmap = new SKBitmap(new SKImageInfo(width, height));  // ← never disposed
using var diffPixmap = diffBitmap.PeekPixels();
// ...
return SKImage.FromBitmap(diffBitmap);
```

`SKImage.FromBitmap` copies the pixel data into an immutable image. The source `diffBitmap` should be disposed after. This is pre-existing in `GenerateDifferenceMask` and was copied into `GenerateDifferenceImage`.

**Fix:** Wrap with `using var diffBitmap = ...` or dispose after `SKImage.FromBitmap`.

#### 3. `ErrorPixelPercentage` / `MeanAbsoluteError` / `MeanSquaredError` divide by zero

The constructor is public, so `new SKPixelComparisonResult(0, 0, 0, 0)` produces `NaN` for all computed properties (division by `TotalPixels` which is 0). While `Compare` always produces valid TotalPixels, the public constructor allows this.

**Recommendation:** Either add a guard (`TotalPixels > 0`) in the constructor, or handle 0 in the computed properties (return 0 when TotalPixels is 0, like PSNR handles MSE=0).

---

### 🟡 API Design Issues

#### 4. Tolerance semantics differ between mask-based and threshold-based comparison

- **Threshold (`int tolerance`)**: Per-pixel, all-or-nothing. If `|ΔR|+|ΔG|+|ΔB| ≤ tolerance`, the **entire pixel** (all channels) is excluded from all metrics.
- **Mask (`SKImage mask`)**: Per-channel. Each channel is independently checked against the mask value; a pixel can have some channels counted and others not.

This means SSE from mask-based comparison isn't directly comparable to threshold-based comparison even with equivalent settings. This is by design, but should be explicitly documented.

#### 5. No tolerance-aware `GenerateDifferenceMask` / `GenerateDifferenceImage`

Users can `Compare` with tolerance, but can't visualize which pixels *exceed* that tolerance. Adding overloads like `GenerateDifferenceMask(first, second, tolerance)` would complete the API surface.

#### 6. `SKPixelComparisonResult` could be a sealed class (or readonly record)

The class isn't sealed, allowing subclassing that could break metric property semantics. Since it's a pure data holder with no virtual members, sealing it communicates intent and enables JIT optimizations.

#### 7. Missing MSE from Blazor sample dashboard

The sample shows MAE, RMSE, NRMSE, PSNR but skips MSE and SSE. Consider showing MSE at minimum since RMSE is derived from it — users seeing RMSE without MSE may not understand the relationship.

---

### �� Documentation Issues

#### 8. "How It Works" section doesn't mention squared error tracking

The section explains absolute error accumulation step-by-step but doesn't mention that squared errors are also accumulated in the same pass. Since SSE drives 4 of the 6 new metrics (MSE, RMSE, NRMSE, PSNR), it should be mentioned.

#### 9. Tolerance docs don't explain metric exclusion behavior

The docs say "Pixels where the total per-channel difference is at or below the tolerance are not counted as errors" — but don't clarify that excluded pixels are removed from **all** metrics (AbsoluteError, SSE, etc.), not just ErrorPixelCount. A user might expect tolerance only affects the pixel count.

#### 10. Input overloads table has awkward wording

> "The same overloads are available for `GenerateDifferenceMask` (without mask support) and for the three-argument masked `Compare`."

This doesn't mention `GenerateDifferenceImage` having the same overloads, and "(without mask support)" is confusing in context. Suggest:

> "The same input type overloads are available for `GenerateDifferenceMask`, `GenerateDifferenceImage`, mask-based `Compare`, and tolerance-based `Compare`."

#### 11. No thread-safety documentation

`SKPixelComparer` is a static class with no shared state — it's inherently thread-safe. This is worth stating in the class-level XML doc, especially for CI/test parallelization scenarios.

---

### ✅ What Looks Good

- **Metric formulas are all correct** — MAE, MSE, RMSE, NRMSE, PSNR match standard definitions
- **`SumSquaredError` using `long`** prevents overflow (max possible: `65025 × 3 × pixels`, needs >9.4B pixels to overflow long)
- **Backward-compatible constructor** — 3-param chains to 4-param with `sumSquaredError: 0`
- **Tolerance boundary tests are thorough** — tolerance 8 vs 9 boundary, tolerance 764 vs 765 boundary, zero-tolerance equivalence
- **Blazor sample is well-structured** — image caching, proper Dispose, responsive layout, real-time tolerance slider
- **XML docs are comprehensive** — every public member documented with parameter descriptions and exception docs

### Summary

| Severity | Count | Key Items |
|---|---|---|
| 🔴 Bug | 3 | int overflow (#1), memory leak (#2), divide-by-zero (#3) |
| 🟡 API | 4 | Tolerance semantics (#4), missing overloads (#5), sealed (#6), sample (#7) |
| 🟢 Docs | 4 | SSE docs (#8), tolerance behavior (#9), overloads table (#10), thread-safety (#11) |

**Recommendation:** Fix #1 and #2 before merge (both are straightforward). #3 is lower priority. The API and docs items can be addressed in this PR or tracked as follow-ups.

comments.author: mattleibow
comments.body:
## Blazor Sample Screenshots — Verified ✅

All Blazor sample pages load and render correctly with no console errors. The **Pixel Comparer** page was updated with:
- **SSE** (Sum of Squared Errors) metric added to the metrics grid
- **ChannelCount** metric added showing 3 (RGB) or 4 (RGBA)
- Metrics reorganized into a clean 3-row grid layout

### Pages Verified
| Page | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| Home | ✅ Pass | Renders correctly |
| Shapes | ✅ Pass | Renders correctly |
| Path Interpolation | ✅ Pass | Renders correctly |
| BlurHash | ✅ Pass | Renders correctly |
| **Pixel Comparer** | ✅ Pass | **Updated** — new SSE/ChannelCount metrics, 3-row grid |

> Screenshots captured with Playwright (headless Chromium + WebGL) against `dotnet run --project samples/SkiaSharpDemo.Blazor`. No console errors on any page.
